final_contract.trigger: When a workflow requires tool execution based on specific use cases.

use_case.description: The specific use case for which tools are being discovered.

session_id.description: The session ID for the current workflow.

tools.description: A list of available tools with their slugs and input schemas.

preconditions: Rube MCP must be connected.
Active connection via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with the appropriate toolkit.
postconditions: Tools are discovered and ready for execution.
Session ID is reused for subsequent operations.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of discovering and executing tools across various parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When a specific task needs to be automated using available tools.

use_case.description: The specific task to be automated.

session.description: The session ID for the current automation context.

available_tools.description: A list of tools available for the specified task.

preconditions: An existing session ID is available.
The specific task to be automated is defined.
postconditions: A list of available tools for the specified task is generated.
The session remains active for further automation steps.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of discovering available tools for various automation tasks.

final_contract.trigger: When the agent needs to find a specific object in the environment.

target_object.description: The name of the object to locate.

suspected_receptacle.description: The receptacle where the object is likely to be found.

object_location.description: The location of the target object if found, or a note of its absence.

preconditions: The agent has identified the target object and its suspected location.
The suspected receptacle is accessible.
postconditions: The agent has either located the target object or confirmed its absence.
The agent may need to take further actions based on the object's status.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of locating a target object within a suspected receptacle, observed across multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When a user needs to extract frames or keyframes from video files.

input_video_file.description: The video file (MP4, MKV, AVI, MOV, etc.) from which frames will be extracted.

extracted_frames.description: A list of extracted frames saved as images.

preconditions: FFmpeg installed and available in PATH
postconditions: Frames are successfully extracted and saved as images.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates the common functionality of extracting frames from video files across multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When creating a mapping from power system bus numbers to 0-indexed array positions.

buses.description: Array of bus data where each row contains bus information.

n_bus.description: Total number of buses in the system.

bus_num_to_idx.description: Mapping of bus numbers to their corresponding 0-indexed positions.

preconditions: The input 'buses' must be a valid array with bus data.
The input 'n_bus' must be a positive integer.
postconditions: A mapping from bus numbers to 0-indexed positions is created successfully.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of mapping bus numbers to indices observed across multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When migrating applications from Spring Boot 2.x to 3.x or updating security configurations.

migration_type.description: The type of migration being performed (e.g., Spring Boot, Spring Security).

commands.description: A list of commands to execute for the migration process.

preconditions: The application is using Spring Boot 2.x or Spring Security 5.
The user has access to the necessary migration guides and resources.
postconditions: The application is updated to Spring Boot 3.x or Spring Security 6.
All migration commands have been executed successfully.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common migration commands needed across different Spring Boot and Spring Security upgrades.

final_contract.trigger: When the agent needs to cool an object using a specific appliance.

object.description: The identifier of the object to be cooled.

temperature_receptacle.description: The identifier of the appliance used for cooling (e.g., fridge).

target_receptacle.description: The final location where the cooled object will be placed.

result.description: Confirmation that the object has been cooled and is ready for the next task.

preconditions: The agent is holding the object to be cooled.
The temperature receptacle is accessible.
postconditions: The object is in a cooled state.
The object is placed in the target receptacle.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of cooling an object using an appliance, observed across multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When executing complex workflows involving multiple operations across different systems.

use_case.description: The specific use case for which tools are being searched.

tool_slug.description: The slug of the tool to be executed.

arguments.description: Schema-compliant arguments for the tool execution.

response.description: The response from the executed tools.

preconditions: Relevant tools have been discovered using RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS.
Prerequisite steps have been executed before the main operations.
postconditions: Data has been passed between steps using tool responses.
Bulk operations or data processing have been completed using RUBE_REMOTE_WORKBENCH.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common workflow structure observed across multiple parent skills for executing complex operations.

final_contract.trigger: When analyzing audio files for energy patterns or detecting pauses and silence.

energies.description: Path to energy JSON file containing calculated energy values

preconditions: Input audio file exists
Output path is writable
postconditions: Energy JSON file is created
Energy values are calculated and stored
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of calculating energy from audio files, which is essential for both pause and silence detection.

final_contract.trigger: When creating or updating a skill that requires a structured skill file.

topic_slug.description: The slugified name of the skill topic.

topic_name.description: The display name of the skill topic.

skill_description.description: A description of what the skill does and when to use it.

skill_file.description: The generated SKILL.md file content in markdown format.

preconditions: The skill name and description must be defined.
The topic slug must be unique and valid.
postconditions: A structured SKILL.md file is created.
The skill file is ready for further editing or packaging.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates the process of generating a structured skill file from defined inputs, which is a common requirement across multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When processing data for validation or transformation tasks.

input.description: Input file or target path to process

result.description: Path to the output file or results

preconditions: Input file must exist and be accessible
Output path must be writable
postconditions: Processed results are saved to the specified output path
Data quality checks are performed on the output
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates common data processing tasks observed in both data engineering and data science workflows.

final_contract.trigger: When developing or reviewing Python tests for Temporal workflows or general Python applications.

test_type.description: The type of test to be executed (e.g., unit, integration, end-to-end).

resource_file.description: The specific resource file to load based on the testing needs.

test_result.description: Indicates whether the test passed or failed.

preconditions: The testing environment is properly set up.
The necessary resource files are accessible.
postconditions: Test results are recorded.
Any necessary cleanup is performed.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates common testing strategies and resource loading for both Temporal workflows and general Python testing.

final_contract.trigger: When analyzing network traffic for potential threats such as port scans or DoS attacks.

tcp_packets.description: List of TCP packets to analyze for port scanning.

packets_per_minute_avg.description: Average packets per minute for DoS detection.

packets_per_minute_max.description: Maximum packets per minute for DoS detection.

has_port_scan.description: Indicates if a port scan has been detected.

has_dos.description: Indicates if a DoS pattern has been detected.

preconditions: tcp_packets must be provided for port scan detection.
packets_per_minute_avg and packets_per_minute_max must be provided for DoS detection.
postconditions: has_port_scan indicates the presence of a port scan.
has_dos indicates the presence of a DoS attack.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates the detection logic for both port scans and DoS attacks, which are common analyses in network traffic evaluation.

final_contract.trigger: When a user needs to create, edit, or analyze .docx or .xlsx files.

office_file.description: The input Office file (.docx or .xlsx) to be processed.

output_directory.description: The directory where unpacked files will be stored.

processed_file.description: The output Office file after processing.

preconditions: The input file must be a valid .docx or .xlsx file.
The output directory must be writable.
postconditions: The processed Office file is created in the specified output directory.
The original file is preserved.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedures for processing Office files across both .docx and .xlsx parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When audio needs to be extracted from a video file.

duration.description: Optional duration limit in seconds (default: full video)

audio_file.description: Extracted audio file in WAV format

preconditions: Input video file exists
Output path is writable
postconditions: Audio file is created in the specified output path
Audio file is in WAV format
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common functionality of extracting audio from video files observed in both parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When analyzing media file properties such as duration, resolution, and bitrate.

media_info.description: Structured information about the media file including duration, resolution, bitrate, codec, and stream details.

preconditions: The input file must be a valid media file format (e.g., .mp4, .mkv, .avi).
postconditions: The media information is successfully extracted and returned in JSON format.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates common media analysis tasks observed in multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When exporting geometries for use in other 3D software.

mesh.description: The Three.js mesh object to be exported.

filepath.description: The file path where the exported OBJ data will be saved.

objData.description: The exported OBJ data as a string.

preconditions: The mesh must have a valid geometry.
The mesh must be part of a Three.js scene.
postconditions: The OBJ file is written to the specified filepath.
The exported OBJ data is returned.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of exporting geometries in world coordinates across multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When an object needs to be cleaned before proceeding with a task.

object.description: The object that needs to be cleaned.

receptacle.description: The cleaning receptacle to be used (e.g., sinkbasin).

result.description: Confirmation that the object is now clean.

preconditions: The object must be in the agent's inventory.
The agent must be at the cleaning receptacle.
postconditions: The object is in a clean state.
The agent can proceed with the next task step.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common cleaning procedure observed in both parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When validating the presence of transits in light curves.

preprocessing_method.description: Method used for preprocessing the light curve data.

period_range.description: List of periods to check for potential transits.

duration_range.description: List of durations to check for potential transits.

validation_results.description: Results of the validation process, including potential issues and recommendations.

preconditions: Light curve data is available for analysis.
Preprocessing has been applied to the light curve data.
postconditions: Validation results are generated and available for review.
Recommendations for further analysis are provided.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates common validation procedures for transit signals observed in both parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When computing power flows in a power system using branch data.

branch_data.description: JSON object containing branch data including F_BUS, T_BUS, BR_R, BR_X, BR_B, RATE_A, RATE_B, RATE_C, TAP, SHIFT, BR_STATUS, ANGMIN, ANGMAX.

bus_data.description: JSON object containing bus data including generation and load information.

power_flows.description: JSON object containing computed power flows for each branch.

preconditions: Branch data and bus data are provided in the correct format.
The system is in a steady state with known voltage magnitudes and angles.
postconditions: Power flows for each branch are computed and returned.
Nodal balance equations are satisfied.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure for computing power flows using branch and bus data across different power flow models.

final_contract.trigger: When assessing the performance of software applications.

target.description: Target path to analyze or process

performance_metrics.description: Performance metrics including latency, throughput, and resource usage

preconditions: Performance metrics must be defined
Target application must be accessible for analysis
postconditions: Performance metrics are generated and available for review
Any performance issues are identified
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates performance assessment procedures observed in both Java and security contexts.

final_contract.trigger: When training machine learning models on cloud GPUs.

gpu_type.description: The type of GPU to use for training (e.g., A100, T4).

timeout.description: The maximum time allowed for the training process in seconds.

loss.description: The loss value returned after training.

preconditions: Modal app is set up with the appropriate image and dependencies installed.
The training function is defined and ready to be invoked.
postconditions: The training process completes and returns the loss value.
Results are printed to the console.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of training models on GPUs using Modal, which is shared across both parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When audio loudness needs to be adjusted to a specific target level.

input_file.description: The input audio file to be normalized.

target_loudness.description: The target loudness level in LUFS.

true_peak.description: The target true peak level in dBTP.

preconditions: Input audio file exists and is accessible.
Target loudness, true peak, and optionally LRA are specified.
postconditions: Output audio file is created with adjusted loudness.
Loudness meets specified target levels.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common audio normalization procedures observed in both parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When invoking the Transit Least Squares algorithm for exoplanet transit detection.

flux_err.description: List of flux uncertainties corresponding to the flux data.

folded_phase.description: Phase-folded data for the detected transits.

folded_y.description: Flux values corresponding to the folded phase.

model_phase.description: Model phase for the phase-folded data.

model_flux.description: Model flux values corresponding to the model phase.

preconditions: Flux data and corresponding flux uncertainties are provided.
The Transit Least Squares algorithm is being used.
postconditions: Phase-folded data is computed and available for analysis.
Model phase and model flux are generated.
final_contract.rationale: The requirement for flux uncertainties is consistently emphasized across multiple parent skills, making it a coherent child skill.

final_contract.trigger: When calculating branch susceptances for power flow analysis.

branches.description: List of branch data containing reactance values.

bus_num_to_idx.description: Mapping of bus numbers to their corresponding indices.

branch_susceptances.description: List of calculated susceptances for each branch.

preconditions: Branches data must be available and correctly formatted.
Bus number to index mapping must be established.
postconditions: Susceptances for all branches are calculated and stored.
Mapping of bus numbers to indices is maintained.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of calculating branch susceptances from reactance values observed in multiple parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When configuring the detail level for image analysis tasks.

image_url.description: The URL of the image to be analyzed.

task_type.description: The type of task for which detail level is being configured.

detail.description: The recommended detail level for the image analysis.

preconditions: The image URL must be valid and accessible.
The task type must be recognized by the system.
postconditions: The detail level is set based on the task type.
The system is ready to process the image with the configured detail level.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates the detail level configuration logic observed in both parent skills.

final_contract.trigger: When analyzing audio/video for pauses or silence segments.

output.description: Path to output JSON file containing detected segments

preconditions: Energy JSON file is available
Output path is specified
postconditions: Output JSON file is created with detected segments
Analysis of audio/video is completed
final_contract.rationale: Both parent skills require processing an energy JSON file and produce an output JSON file, making this a coherent child skill.

final_contract.trigger: When setting up the environment for document or presentation processing.

file_type.description: The type of Office file being processed (docx or pptx).

dependencies.description: A list of required dependencies for the specified Office file type.

preconditions: The user has access to the required package managers (npm, pip, apt-get).
The user has internet access to install dependencies.
postconditions: The environment is set up with the necessary dependencies for processing Office files.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill consolidates the dependency requirements for both document and presentation processing into a single reusable contract.

final_contract.trigger: When the agent needs to find a specific object whose location is unknown.

target_object.description: The name of the object to locate (e.g., 'potato').

location.description: The location where the target object has been found.

preconditions: The target object is not in the agent's inventory.
The location of the target object is unknown.
postconditions: The target object has been located.
The agent can proceed to take the target object.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common procedure of locating a specific object in various receptacles.

final_contract.trigger: When implementing control systems to prevent integral windup during saturation.

max_integral.description: The maximum allowable value for the integral term.

u_I.description: The current value of the integral term to be clamped.

clamped_u_I.description: The clamped value of the integral term after applying limits.

preconditions: The control system output is saturated.
The integral term is being used in the control calculation.
postconditions: The integral term is limited to prevent windup.
The clamped value of the integral term is returned.
final_contract.rationale: This child skill encapsulates the common anti-windup strategy used in both parent skills.
